key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?

proof_gap: The boot verb's own friction earmarks its closure at issue #6 (shell+health), not #5, so the contract does not disambiguate which of #5/#6 owns boot, nor how a long-running watch/dev process reconciles with the run-once verdict+evidence model.
suggested_closure: Plan/decider confirms whether #5 wires boot.maps_to to a Prototype-0 dev command (data-only) now and #6 rewires it to the real app-serve+health, and defines how boot handles a long-running process.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Research inferred that npm run typecheck (wrapped by ./harness verify) is the Prototype-0 validation command, and that verify's 'degraded' verdict satisfies AC 'runs and passes' because degraded is non-blocking (exit 0).
proof_gap: AC wording says the validation command must 'run and pass', but verify returns 'degraded' (not 'pass') while lint/test/build stay unknown; the contract cannot prove whether degraded==passes for this AC or whether #5 must add a linter/test to reach pass (which would strain ADR-0002's no-speculative-frameworks constraint).
suggested_closure: Plan/decider confirms typecheck/verify (degraded, exit 0) satisfies the validation AC at Prototype 0, or scopes minimal lint/test wiring; record the interpretation in the plan.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Issue #5 confirms ./harness verify (wraps npm run typecheck) is the Prototype-0 validation command and that its degraded/exit-0 verdict satisfies the 'runs and passes' AC (non-blocking).
proof_gap: verify stays degraded because lint/test/build are unknown; degraded (not pass) is the honest accepted baseline and only fail blocks (exit 1).

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Issue #5 keeps boot unknown; the Prototype-0 dev inner loop is npm run dev (tsc --noEmit --watch), run directly, not mapped to boot.
proof_gap: A long-running interactive dev/serve process does not fit the harness run-once verdict+evidence model, so boot.maps_to stays null at #5.
suggested_closure: Owned by #6: #6 wraps the interactive dev/serve + health (exec/handoff or readiness-probe+detach) and rewires boot.maps_to in contract.yml; the dev inner loop today is npm run dev.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Prior friction (entries #8/#14) inferred that a long-running interactive dev/watch process could not fit the harness run-once verdict+evidence model, so npm run dev was run directly, outside the harness.
proof_gap: The harness had no way to invoke a long-running interactive process; ./harness boot stayed unknown and docs pointed users at npm run dev directly, so AC3 (dev invokable through the harness CLI) was unmet (review F-01).
suggested_closure: Closed at #5 by the mode: exec interactive/handoff verb category (ADR-0004 / CORE-COMPONENT-0003 R17): ./harness dev now execs npm run dev via process handoff, so the dev command is genuinely invokable through the harness. boot (app-serve + health) remains deferred to #6.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Research inferred that issue #6 must (a) choose a TypeScript-at-runtime execution strategy to run a real HTTP server under the tsc --noEmit baseline (Node 22.17.1 supports 'node --experimental-strip-types', verified on disk, as the no-emit/no-dependency path), (b) implement the HTTP server with Node's built-in 'http' module rather than adding a web framework, and (c) define how boot's long-running app-serve process reconciles with the run-once verdict+evidence model (reuse ADR-0004 mode: exec handoff vs. readiness-probe+detach).
proof_gap: ADR-0004/Decision #40 reserve boot for #6 (boot.maps_to: null, verdict unknown) but the contract does not prove which HTTP mechanism, which TS runtime-execution path, or which boot lifecycle model #6 should use; ADR-0002 forbids frameworks and any build beyond tsc but does not name the runtime path, so it is inferred not proven.
suggested_closure: Plan/decider selects the HTTP mechanism (built-in http vs framework, likely via a new ADR-0005), the TS runtime-execution strategy and its ADR-0002 impact, and the boot lifecycle (mode: exec handoff vs readiness-probe+detach) before implementation; then wire boot.maps_to in contract.yml (data-only per R8).

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Research inferred that Node's built-in 'node:test' runner (zero new dependency, ships with Node 22) is the honest Prototype-0 choice for the issue-required health-endpoint unit/integration check, and that issue #6 may now wire the harness 'test' verb (currently unknown) to move it toward pass.
proof_gap: test.maps_to is null and no test runner or npm test script exists; ADR-0002 discourages speculative frameworks, and prior friction deferred 'test' beyond #5, so whether #6 wires 'test' (and with node:test vs a framework) is inferred, not proven by the contract.
suggested_closure: Plan/decider confirms node:test as the dependency-light Prototype-0 test runner and whether #6 wires test.maps_to (and adds an npm test script) as part of closing the health-endpoint test requirement.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Research inferred that AC3's 'documented dev command' refers to the app-serve+health boot #6 introduces (surfaced through the harness, e.g. ./harness boot), not the existing ./harness dev (mode: exec) which execs 'npm run dev' = tsc --noEmit --watch (a typecheck inner loop, not an app server).
proof_gap: The repo already has a 'documented dev command' (./harness dev / npm run dev) that starts a typecheck watch, not a serving app; the contract/docs do not prove whether #6 changes what dev does or introduces a distinct serve entry point, so the AC3-to-verb mapping is inferred.
suggested_closure: Plan/decider defines which documented command starts the shell+health (reuse/extend npm run dev, add npm run start/serve, and/or wire boot) and updates README + contract.yml so AC3 is genuinely invokable through the harness.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Research friction #16 inferred boot's app-serve lifecycle (HTTP mechanism, TS runtime-execution path, and boot handoff model); #6/ADR-0005 now resolves it.
proof_gap: boot.maps_to was null (owned by #6) and the contract did not prove which HTTP mechanism / TS runtime path / boot lifecycle to use; it was inferred not proven.
suggested_closure: Resolved by #6/ADR-0005 (D1/D2/D4): boot wired mode: exec -> npm run start (node --experimental-strip-types src/main.ts; node:http shell + /health). Data-only contract edit per CC-0003 R8/R17; introspect via boot --print/--json.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Research friction #17 inferred node:test as the dependency-light Prototype-0 runner and that #6 may wire the harness test verb (then unknown).
proof_gap: test.maps_to was null with no npm test script or test files; whether/how #6 wires test (node:test vs a framework) was inferred, not proven by the contract.
suggested_closure: Resolved by #6/ADR-0005 (D7): test.maps_to -> npm test runs node:test suites in tests/app/ (node --test --experimental-strip-types, zero deps); test moves unknown -> pass and joins the verify aggregate (verify stays degraded/exit-0).

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Research friction #18 inferred AC3's 'documented dev command' for the shell+health is a new app-serve verb, not the existing ./harness dev (a tsc --noEmit --watch typecheck loop, not a server).
proof_gap: The repo already had ./harness dev (npm run dev = typecheck watch); the contract/docs did not prove whether #6 changes dev or adds a distinct serve entry, so the AC3-to-verb mapping was inferred.
suggested_closure: Resolved by #6/ADR-0005 (D5): the shell+health starts via ./harness boot (npm run start); ./harness dev stays the typecheck watch, documented distinctly in README + .harness/README.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Research for issue #7 inferred that launching one code-server process against a configured PROJECT_PATH is naturally a new harness interactive/handoff verb (mode: exec, reusing the ADR-0004/ADR-0005 boot/dev pattern) executing a small launch script that isolates all code-server-specific flags (PRD 5.7), rather than a bare documented shell command outside the harness.
proof_gap: The .harness/contract.yml has no editor/code-server launch verb, code-server is NOT installed in this devcontainer (command -v code-server = none; not in devcontainer.json features), and doctor only proves Node health, not editor-provider availability; so whether #7 adds a new mode: exec verb (e.g. an 'edit'/'code-server' verb) vs a standalone scripts/ launcher, and how PROJECT_PATH is configured, is inferred not proven.
suggested_closure: Plan/decider chooses where the launch script lives and whether to wire a new mode: exec harness verb (data-only per CC-0003 R8/R17, reusing the boot handoff proven by ADR-0005), keeping code-server-specific arguments isolated (PRD 5.7); if a durable code-server launch/provider-isolation contract is warranted, a small new ADR is proposed (see brief).

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Research for issue #7 inferred that code-server is not available in this environment and must be installed/provisioned before the launcher can be demonstrated; verification of AC (editor reachable, terminal works) therefore relies on manual demonstration, while argument/invalid-path validation is automatable without code-server running.
proof_gap: harness doctor proves only Node/toolchain health (node present, engines range, node_modules), not whether code-server (the first editor provider, PRD 5.7) is installed or launchable; command -v code-server returns nothing and devcontainer.json declares no code-server feature, so editor-provider readiness is unproven by the harness.
suggested_closure: Plan/decider decides how code-server is provisioned (devcontainer feature, install step, or documented prerequisite) and how AC is verified (manual demo for browser/terminal + automatable script-level invalid-path/arg-validation tests); optionally extend doctor to report code-server presence as a readiness diagnostic (degraded, never fail) in a later story.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Plan for issue #7 inferred that adding a NEW mode: exec handoff verb (e.g. 'edit' for the code-server launcher) is NOT purely a contract-data edit like boot/dev were: the harness main() dispatch routes only an allowlisted set of verb NAMES (help|orient|doctor|lint|test|build|boot|dev|verify|status|clean|friction), so a brand-new verb name must also be added to that main() case and to verb_help, while the mode->handler selection (dispatch_verb) remains data-driven.
proof_gap: The research brief and ADR-0005 call boot/dev 'data-only' because those verb NAMES were already present in the main() allowlist; contract.yml cannot express a brand-new verb name, so routing a new verb requires a minimal harness script edit (permitted structural dispatch under CORE-COMPONENT-0003 R8) that the contract alone does not prove.
suggested_closure: Implement stage adds the 'edit' verb name to the harness main() dispatch allowlist + verb_help and sets edit.maps_to + mode: exec in contract.yml; a future harness change could derive the main() routing allowlist from the contract verbs so new mode: exec verbs become fully data-only.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Resolved by #7/ADR-0006: the new mode: exec 'edit' verb-routing gap the Plan inferred is now closed. 'edit' is wired as contract DATA (edit.maps_to: npm run edit, mode: exec, json: true) plus the minimal permitted structural-dispatch edit (added 'edit' to the harness main() allowlist case + one verb_help line, CC-0003 R8); the mode->handler selection stays data-driven via dispatch_verb.
proof_gap: Before #7 the harness main() routed only an allowlisted set of verb NAMES (lint|test|build|boot|dev), and contract.yml cannot introduce a brand-new verb name, so a new mode: exec verb was not purely data-only; ./harness edit --print/--json now prove the routing (resolves 'npm run edit', exit 0, verdict-exempt descriptor) without launching code-server.
suggested_closure: Closed: TEST-34 + TEST-20 in tests/harness/run.sh assert edit's contract values, --print/--json handoff, help listing, and exclusion from run-to-completion enumeration; a future harness change could derive the main() allowlist from the contract verbs so new mode: exec verbs become fully data-only.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Resolved by #7/ADR-0006: the launch-vs-script + PROJECT_PATH-config inference from #7 research is closed. Launching one code-server process against a configured path is a dependency-light POSIX launcher, scripts/launch-editor.sh, surfaced as the provider-agnostic 'edit' mode: exec verb (npm run edit); the folder is configured via PROJECT_PATH and the loopback port via EDITOR_PORT (default 8080), mirroring the ADR-0005 PORT precedent, with ALL code-server flags isolated behind the launcher (PRD 5.7).
proof_gap: Before #7 the contract had no editor/launch verb and it was unproven whether the launcher would be a bare documented command or a harness verb, and how the target path/port were configured; ./harness edit (edit.maps_to: npm run edit) and PROJECT_PATH/EDITOR_PORT now make it explicit and invocable on the single operating surface.
suggested_closure: Closed: contract wires edit -> npm run edit -> sh scripts/launch-editor.sh; README 'Launch the editor (code-server)' + .harness/README document PROJECT_PATH/EDITOR_PORT and the read-only launch; tests/launcher/ (TEST-L1..L8) prove invalid-path/isolation/no-mutation.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Resolved by #7/ADR-0006: the code-server-availability inference from #7 research is closed as a documented-prerequisite decision. code-server is NOT bundled and is absent here/CI, so AC1-AC3 (editor reachable, folder opens, integrated terminal) are verified by a manual demo capturing the startup command + duration, while AC4-AC5 (invalid-path fail-fast + read-only/no-mutation) are verified by code-server-free node:test tests (tests/launcher/, a stub code-server on PATH) wired into verify; the launcher fails fast with clear guidance when code-server is absent.
proof_gap: harness doctor proves only Node/toolchain health, not editor-provider readiness (command -v code-server = none); extending doctor to report code-server presence as a degraded-never-fail readiness diagnostic is deferred to a later story per ADR-0006 D7.
suggested_closure: Closed for #7: code-server documented as a prerequisite in README with install guidance; AC4-AC5 automated (tests/launcher), AC1-AC3 manual (project/issues/7/implementation/README.md). Deferred: a future story may add a doctor code-server readiness check (degraded, never fail).

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Issue #8 (verify direct filesystem editing) research must infer that AC1 (edited file shows identical change on disk) and AC2 (stopping the editor leaves files unchanged) can only be proven by a MANUAL demo on a code-server-provisioned host, and that code-server edits its opened folder IN PLACE (no copy/import), because the launcher opens the real PROJECT_PATH directory. AC3 (filesystem permission behaviour) and file ownership are inferred from a manual observation, not proven by any harness verb.
proof_gap: ./harness doctor proves only Node/toolchain health, not editor-provider readiness (command -v code-server = none in this devcontainer/CI), and there is no harness verb that opens code-server and observes on-disk edits, process-stop file integrity, or resulting permission/ownership. The automated launcher suite (tests/launcher TEST-L1..L8) proves the LAUNCHER's own zero-mutation footprint, but cannot prove that a USER edit inside code-server lands on the same inode, since it never runs the real editor.
suggested_closure: Verify #8 AC1-AC3 by a documented manual demonstration (edit a file in code-server, diff the host path, stop the editor, confirm files intact, record ls -l permissions + ownership and the code-server workspace-state location) captured under project/issues/8/implementation; AC4 (no project-dir mutation) reuses the ADR-0006 read-only launcher guarantee + tests/launcher snapshot. A future story may add a doctor code-server readiness diagnostic (degraded, never fail).

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Research for #9 (capture startup and resource measurements) had to infer that baseline measurement requires a transiently-provisioned code-server, matching #7: code-server is a documented prerequisite that is absent here (launch-editor.sh exits 127 when command -v code-server is empty), so the launcher cannot be started in this environment to capture startup duration, memory, or idle CPU. The concrete evidence dimensions (PRD 18) and the deliverable path (a Prototype 0 measurements doc under docs/) were also inferred, not proven by any harness verb.
proof_gap: No harness verb proves editor-provider readiness or captures runtime resource metrics: doctor reports only Node/toolchain health and is silent on code-server; there is no capture/measure verb, so startup/memory/idle-CPU/version/storage-path evidence must be gathered manually against a transiently-installed code-server.
suggested_closure: Plan should specify the transient provisioning + manual measurement protocol (reuse #7 approach) and the docs/ evidence-document path; a future story MAY add a doctor code-server readiness diagnostic (degraded, never fail) per ADR-0006 D7 deferral.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Resolved by #9: baseline startup/resource measurement required a TRANSIENTLY-provisioned code-server (4.129.0 into /tmp/cs via the standalone installer, never a repo dependency) because no harness verb proves editor-provider readiness. Measured one idle session through the sanctioned seam (PROJECT_PATH=/tmp/demo-proj-9 EDITOR_PORT=8129 ./harness edit): startup ~0.65s wall-clock to port bind, code-server node tree RSS ~153 MiB, idle CPU ~0.0%, version 4.129.0, storage under /home/vscode/.local/share/code-server (extensions/ + User/); restart re-binds cleanly with state persisted, invalid PROJECT_PATH fails read-only non-zero, kill -9 frees the port with no supervision (ADR-0006 D6).
proof_gap: No harness verb captures runtime resource metrics and none proves code-server presence: ./harness edit exits 127 when code-server is absent, so startup/memory/idle-CPU/version/storage evidence had to be gathered manually against a transiently-installed code-server; doctor is silent on editor-provider readiness.
suggested_closure: Closed for #9: evidence recorded in docs/prototype-0/startup-and-resource-measurements.md (linked from docs/README.md). A future story MAY add a doctor code-server-readiness diagnostic (degraded, never fail) and/or a capture/measure verb per the ADR-0006 D7 deferral.

key_question: What did the agent have to infer that the harness should have proved?
inference: Resolved by #9: the concrete PRD 18 evidence DIMENSIONS (startup command+duration, one-session memory+idle CPU, version+extension/workspace-state storage paths, restart/invalid-path/crash behaviour) and the durable DELIVERABLE PATH (docs/prototype-0/startup-and-resource-measurements.md) were inferred by planning, not proven by any harness verb, and are now realised as a single-session Prototype-0 baseline document discoverable from docs/README.md.
proof_gap: No harness verb enumerates the required evidence dimensions or the docs/ deliverable location; there is no capture/measure verb, so the evidence set and its home under docs/ were inferred from PRD 18/25/29 and ADR-0006 rather than proved.
suggested_closure: Closed for #9: the document exists with sections mapped 1:1 to AC1-AC4 plus an environment section, feeding the Prototype 0 review (PRD 29 item 7).
